examples.label: Variants
examples.description: Primary, secondary, and ghost buttons provide different levels of visual emphasis.

examples.label: Sizes
examples.description: Small and default sizes. Use data-size="sm" for compact variant.

examples.label: Leading icon
examples.description: Add a leading icon with data-leading-icon. Left padding is automatically reduced.

examples.label: Trailing icon
examples.description: Add a trailing icon with data-trailing-icon. Right padding is automatically reduced.

examples.label: Icon only
examples.description: Square buttons with only an icon. Use data-icon-only and always provide an aria-label.

examples.label: Destructive
examples.description: Use data-btn-color="danger" on the button or a parent to signal destructive actions (maps to Figma's Destructive=True).

examples.label: Disabled
examples.description: Disabled buttons use a dedicated fill and text color — not opacity.

examples.label: Color palettes
examples.description: Wrap buttons in a container with data-btn-color to override the accent.
